mission.title: opentelemetry: Working Group: Add sustainability Metrics to Semantic Conventions
mission.description: Working Group: Add sustainability Metrics to Semantic Conventions. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current opentelemetry deployment
steps.description:
Verify your opentelemetry version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry
helm list -n opentelemetry 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working opentelemetry installation.

steps.title: Review opentelemetry configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentelemetry configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentelemetry -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry
kubectl get configmap -n opentelemetry -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentelemetry
```
With the introduction of various [European Laws regarding sustainability](https://www.consilium.europa.eu/en/policies/green-deal/fit-for-55-the-eu-plan-for-a-green-transition/), it is becoming increasingly more important for companies to track the

steps.title: Apply the fix for Working Group: Add sustainability Metrics to Semantic…
steps.description:
This proposes a project and working group for establishing Semantic Conventions for sustainability. 

We aim to unify and and establish standards for monitoring sustainability metrics

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry
kubectl get events -n opentelemetry --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Working Group: Add sustainability Metrics to Semantic…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This proposes a project and working group for establishing Semantic Conventions for sustainability. 

We aim to unify and and establish standards for monitoring sustainability metrics

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentelemetry installed or the issue environment reproducible.
